mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales reflects strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms; higher 3-month growth in RSAFS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS snapshot value was 408039.0
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or produces a rolling 3-year Rank IC below 0.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening income growth and recession-risk repricing; when UNRATE increases materially over three months, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE snapshot value was 8.1
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered return is not below unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates rise while consumer prices are still accelerating, equity valuations face both higher discount rates and margin pressure; joint increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp over 6 months and CPIAUCSL 6-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS snapshot value was 0.14
fact:latest available CPIAUCSL snapshot value was 229.177
regime_hint: monetary tightening with positive inflation impulse
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening-inflation trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of occurrences or if the interaction term has nonnegative predictive slope in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production strength confirmed by payroll expansion indicates broad real-economy momentum and improving earnings breadth; simultaneous increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO snapshot value was 96.5685
fact:latest available PAYEMS snapshot value was 132989.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered joint INDPRO and PAYEMS expansions do not outperform unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered returns are negative in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening durable demand, credit creation, and construction activity; when HOUST falls materially below its recent trend, 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST snapshot value was 654.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or if average triggered return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation deceleration with no offsetting rise in the policy rate reduces discount-rate pressure and supports valuation multiples; falling CPIAUCSL momentum with stable FEDFUNDS predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth decelerates versus prior 3 months and FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0.10pp
failure_condition: Reject if the disinflation-stable-policy trigger produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of cases or if its mean return is statistically indistinguishable from unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth accompanied by a falling unemployment rate signals improving labor income without immediate recession stress; concurrent PAYEMS acceleration and UNRATE decline predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds its prior 6-month average and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth acceleration signals stronger aggregate demand and improving earnings capacity; when GDPC1 growth exceeds its prior quarterly pace, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are positive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth rate exceeds previous quarterly growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 snapshot value was 13502.4
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth acceleration is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations or if triggered mean return is below the unconditional mean.
